key_insight: Atomic cluster expansion (ACE) potentials can substantially improve screw-dislocation predictions in bcc refractory metals, but their transferability depends strongly on both training data coverage and element-specific energetics.
engineering_relevance: These models enable more reliable atomistic prediction of plasticity and flow stress in refractory metals, which is important for designing high-temperature structural materials.
method: Extended an existing DFT database to train a family of ACE potentials for V, Nb, Ta, Mo, and W.
Benchmarked the models against screw-dislocation properties, including Peierls barriers and glide behavior.
Used the best-performing models to compute kink-pair activation enthalpies and compare them with Kocks' law and a line-tension model.
evidence_signal: The ACE potentials achieved near-DFT accuracy for Mo and W and substantial improvement for V, Nb, and Ta, with reliable kink-pair enthalpies for Nb, Mo, and W.
watch_item: A single-humped Peierls barrier is not sufficient to validate dislocation-glide fidelity, so database composition and glide-plane prediction must also be checked.
paper_title: Atomic Cluster Expansion Potentials for Screw Dislocations in BCC Refractory Metals
